name: Hessian spectroscopy: edge re-pinning as the slow mode (Attempt 3)
question: Is the post-drop slow relaxation mode (the lag kernel's tau) the spectral edge re-pinning to the new stability boundary?  AdamW's edge lives on the PRECONDITIONED sharpness S_pre = lam_max(P^-1/2 H P^-1/2), P = sqrt(v_hat)+eps, with momentum-corrected threshold (2+2b1)/((1-b1)*eta) = 38/eta for b1=0.9.

design.resume: floor-ladder trunk checkpoints (G3-replay-validated), const 1.5e-3 to step 3000, bs=48
design.scales: m always (anchor bed; B-axis arms only here).  Second scale filled by the Attempt-2 staging gate BEFORE launch: emergence/crossing -> the bracketing/p>1 scale (eta arms only); sublinear-through-top -> l (eta arms only).  This slot intentionally left to the gate; no other post-hoc freedom.
design.spectral_probe: top-16 Ritz values, Lanczos with full reorthogonalization (CPU-stored basis), m_iters=64 for preconditioned (PRIMARY), 32 for raw H (secondary); HVP via fp32 double-backward (NO autocast, math SDPA backend), averaged over 4 fixed probe batches of bs=48 drawn once from generator seed 424242 (identical batches across all probes/arms at a scale)
design.preconditioner: P built from the optimizer's own exp_avg_sq at probe time with bias correction beta2^t, eps=1e-8, frozen during the probe
design.loss_curve: same dense/tail eval cadence as floor rungs in the SAME run -> tau_loss fitted from this run itself (no cross-run transfer)

metrics.S_pre(dS): lam_max of preconditioned Hessian at probe
metrics.tau_spectral: 1-1/e crossing of monotone-rectified R(dS); bootstrap over probe subsets for CI
metrics.tau_loss: exponential fit r(t)=F+A*exp(-(t-3000)/tau) on the same arm (analyze_floor protocol, r2>=0.6 gate)

verdicts.OFF_REGIME: pre-drop sanity fails: S_pre(0) outside [0.5,1.5]x(38/eta1) on the no-drop control -- edge-pinning frame does not apply on this bed; re-pin account of the slow mode FALSIFIED here (terminal)
verdicts.C_REPIN: sanity passes AND R monotone (Spearman rho>=0.8 over probes with dS<=2.5*tau_loss) AND tau_spectral within factor 3 of tau_loss
verdicts.C_DECOUPLED: sanity passes AND (R < 0.2 throughout dS<=2.5*tau_loss OR tau_spectral/tau_loss outside [0.1,10]) -- loss relaxes without edge motion or vice versa (terminal)
verdicts.AMBIGUOUS: anything else: report as measured, no mechanism claim

secondary_B_axis: only scored if C_REPIN at m: sign(tau_spectral(b12)-tau_spectral(b192)) must match the measured tau_loss ~ B^-0.2 direction (slower at small B); agreement = the B-dependent channel IS spectral; disagreement = the spectral mode is the B-blind channel of the 1A mixture reading
caveats_preregistered: training forward is bf16-autocast but probes are fp32: the probed landscape is the fp32 one (mismatch noted, affects both hypotheses equally)
grad clip 1.0 may bind transiently post-drop; clipping events logged per arm
decoupled weight decay excluded from CE Hessian; adds small -eta*wd*w drift not captured by S_pre
Lanczos ghost control: full reorthogonalization; Ritz residuals logged, probes with res > 0.05*|lam| dropped

continue_close.C_REPIN: CONTINUE major: mechanism identified; lag-kernel clock becomes a spectral statement; B-axis secondary decides which channel is spectral
continue_close.C_DECOUPLED or OFF_REGIME: CLOSE the spectral account (falsified on this bed); slow mode remains phenomenological -- honest terminal state
continue_close.AMBIGUOUS: one refinement round allowed ONLY on probe density/iters (no new arms); then terminal as measured

dof_note: zero formula DOF; pure mechanism measurement (G5 safe)
cost_estimate: per probe ~96 Lanczos iters x 4-batch HVP; m arm ~12-15 min, l arm ~35-45 min; 5 m-arms + 3 second-scale arms ~ 3h single-stream, parallelizable P=3
